High Density Housing / Commercial - Addition / Alteration - ***PART 5& 3***Interior and exterior alterations to this 2 level wood frame existing Church (Synagogue).

The proposed work involves demolition of portions of the existing building at the south end and the north end in order to expand those spaces. 

 The north end of the building including the exterior entrance stair structure is being enlarged and revised to accommodate an elevator and generally to increase the accessibility features.

At the south end of the building there will be an addition for classrooms and offices. A new ramp as a secondary access to the 2nd floor of the building will be installed.

A new terrace is being created on the east side with new doors from the existing Social Hall being added to access the terrace. The heating system is to be revised to convert away from gas furnace to electric heat pumps.   

The existing kitchen is being enlarged. There are three new washrooms, two are replacing existing ones and one is additional. The total will now be 4. No grease laden cooking proposed

All gas is being removed from the site. The kitchen range is to be changed to electric and the heating system is being converted as described. 

the Fire Alarm system is for the Elevator only as the building Occupant Load is programmed for fewer than 300 people and there is no room with an occupant load of more than 150 people. 

Revision 1 - cost cutting amendments made to the scope of the project. They include deletion of the extension to the kitchen, reduction of some landscape elements such as the deletion of pergolas at the gates on Fraser Street and 10th Avenue, the reduction of the raised timber planters, and the thinning of some planting. There is also a change to the fencing related to the PMT. 

Revision 2 - Addition of Elevator Drain, Oil Interceptor, Sump, Sump Pump

Revision 3 - to change the applicant to Chris Boni

projectdescription:
High Density Housing / Commercial - Addition / Alteration - Interior and exterior alterations to replace existing glazed entry doors and provide new security gate at the northeast exit in this existing mixed use residential and commercial building on this site. 
Proposed work includes installation of electronic magnetic locks at the principle entry and installation of new automatic door openers for the residential portions only. 

Building Review Branch Notes:
Proposed electromagnetic locks to comply with Article 3.4.6.16.
Acceptable door release hardware for an electromagnetic lock shall be located in close proximity to the exit door and shall be equipped with
a) a push button together with a motion sensor or a pressure sensitive pad that will immediately release the locking device,
b) a push button that is
i) directly connected to the electrical circuit that provides power to the locking device, without any intervening mechanism,
ii) embossed with the word “EXIT” on the activation surface in text with dimensions of no less than 25 mm,
iii) internally illuminated by a permanent LED type light source, and
iv) labeled “DOOR RELEASE” in plain and legible characters,
c) an electromagnetic lock that
i) will reset automatically, except as provided in (c)(ii),
ii) has an automatic reset feature that is not activated for at least 15 seconds, and
iii) can only be reset by manual means after the activation of the fire alarm system
